## Workflow guidance from active subagents

Each enabled subagent below carries usage instructions for you (the dispatching agent). Read these as workflow rules that apply this iteration — they encode how to USE the subagent and when in your phase to dispatch it.

### blueprint-reviewer

- Dispatch me BEFORE writing any prover objectives or touching Lean files.
  The plan agent's job is to make sure the blueprint is complete and
  detailed enough first; only then should provers be assigned. A weak
  blueprint produces low-quality prover work that the next iter then
  has to throw away.
- I am highly recommended every plan phase. The audit warning fires
  if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.

  **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
    - no chapter under `blueprint/src/chapters/` was edited since my
      prior dispatch (check via `git diff --stat HEAD~N
      blueprint/src/chapters/` where N spans iters back to my last
      run);
    - my prior verdict cleared the HARD GATE for all chapters
      currently under active prover work;
    - no must-fix-this-iter finding from my prior dispatch remains
      live (every flagged chapter has either been writer-patched or
      dropped from objectives).

  Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`
  with the one-line rationale. Do NOT skip me when the prior verdict
  flagged any chapter `partial | false` and that chapter still feeds
  a live prover lane — the HARD GATE depends on a current audit.
- I always read the WHOLE blueprint. Do not pass me a scope-limiting
  directive — even when the iteration's focus is narrow, the cross-
  chapter view is the point of running me.
- Read my per-chapter checklist and use it to decide which chapters
  need a follow-up writer dispatch this iter (consult your catalog
  for the blueprint-writing subagent). You do not need to re-read
  the chapters yourself; the checklist is your view into them.

### HARD GATE — per-file prover dispatch

This is the rule that protects the project from low-quality prover work.
Apply it verbatim, every iter, no exceptions:

- For each `.lean` file F you are considering adding to
  `## Current Objectives` (i.e. about to send a prover to), identify
  the corresponding blueprint chapter C. The mapping is: if some
  chapter declares `% archon:covers ... F ...` near its top, THAT
  chapter is C (a consolidated chapter blueprints several files while
  its siblings are thin pointers); otherwise fall back to the 1:1
  `Foo/Bar.lean → Foo_Bar.tex` slug. Look up C in my per-chapter
  checklist. When a chapter declares `covers:`, its single verdict
  gates EVERY file it lists — so a `correct: false` on the
  consolidated chapter defers all of them, and one writer directive
  against that chapter fixes the content behind all of them.
- If C has `complete: true` AND `correct: true` AND no must-fix-this-iter
  finding touches it, F may go into the objectives.
- Otherwise (C is `partial | false` on either axis, OR a must-fix
  finding names C, OR a broken `\uses{}` in C points at a label F's
  blueprint depends on):
  1. DROP F from this iter's objectives (by default — but see the
     same-iter fast path below).
  2. Dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing subagent for C THIS
     iter with a directive targeting the specific must-fix items I
     flagged.
  3. Record in iter/iter-NNN/plan.md why F was at risk and what the
     writer was asked to fix.
- **Same-iter fast path (sanctioned — removes a wasted iter).** After
  the writer returns AND `lake build` is green, you MAY re-dispatch me
  scoped to C alone — a fresh slug, a directive naming only C and the
  must-fix items I flagged. If that scoped re-review returns C as
  `complete: true` AND `correct: true` with no must-fix finding, the
  gate is satisfied for C: add F to THIS iter's objectives and send a
  prover. No need to burn an iter waiting. If the scoped re-review
  still fails, F stays deferred.
- If you do NOT take the fast path, F simply waits: the next iter's
  mandatory dispatch of me re-confirms C before any prover runs on F.
  The fast path is purely a latency optimization — it never weakens
  the gate. F enters objectives ONLY on a fresh `complete + correct`
  verdict with no must-fix, whether that verdict arrives this iter (via
  the fast path) or next (via the mandatory dispatch). A green build
  alone is NOT sufficient; the re-review is what clears the gate.

### Strategy / multi-route handling

- If the strategy has multiple viable routes and I report that one
  or more routes have no blueprint coverage, dispatch the catalog's
  blueprint-writing subagent (one call per missing-coverage route)
  in the same iteration. Do not let provers begin work on a route
  until its blueprint coverage is in place.
- If I flag a definition that may require a strategy modification,
  treat that as a STRATEGY.md update task before any further Lean work
  — provers cannot be dispatched until the strategy update is reflected
  in the blueprint.

### What "deferred" means in practice

Deferring a prover round is the correct, intentional action — not a
failure. The 1-iter latency cost of waiting for a writer is small
compared to the cost of a prover formalizing a broken blueprint and
the work being thrown away. Log the deferral cleanly in plan.md and
move on; the next iter's mandatory me-dispatch will green-light F.

### mathlib-analogist

- I have two modes. Choose by setting `## Mode: api-alignment` or
  `## Mode: cross-domain-inspiration` in the directive header.
  Dispatch the right mode for the question:

  - **api-alignment**: "should this declaration be a typeclass / a
    structure / bundled / unbundled?", "does Mathlib already do this
    and we're building a parallel?". This is the default and matches
    every trigger below under "Proactive" and "Reactive".
  - **cross-domain-inspiration**: "this categorical pattern has been
    failing for K iters, has Mathlib solved the SAME structural
    problem in a different mathematical area?". Use the cross-domain
    triggers below.

- In api-alignment mode, I tell you whether Mathlib already does the
  same thing the right way and what the cost of NOT aligning would
  be. In cross-domain-inspiration mode, I bring back structural
  analogues (with citations) and the technique each used, so you
  can port it.

### Proactive triggers (BEFORE the design ships)

Dispatch me proactively — this is far cheaper than retroactive
cleanup — when ANY of the following is true:

- You are about to write a new infrastructure definition into the
  blueprint or have a blueprint-writing subagent (per your catalog)
  write one. Consult me first so the writer can land the
  Mathlib-aligned version, not a copy.
- You are about to add a new declaration to PROGRESS.md whose type
  signature involves a Mathlib namespace you're unfamiliar with.
  Treat me as a sanity check on the signature shape.
- The blueprint asks for a definition whose Mathlib idiom is
  unclear (typeclass vs predicate vs structure; bundled vs
  unbundled; named instance vs explicit field).

### Reactive triggers (when something already went wrong)

Dispatch me when:

- Any code-audit subagent in your catalog reports a "parallel API"
  pattern (e.g. the project defines `Scheme.HModule` by copy-and-
  modify from a Mathlib AddCommGrp version).
- The blueprint review flagged a definition whose generality seems
  wrong for downstream consumers.
- **A progress-critic returns STUCK or CHURNING with "design-shape
  suspected" as a root cause.** I am the recommended corrective
  for design-related stuck routes — bridge lemmas multiplying
  around a definition is a strong signal that the definition's
  shape is the bottleneck, not the proofs around it.

### Cross-domain inspiration triggers

Dispatch me in **cross-domain-inspiration** mode when:

- A specific tactic, categorical pattern, or proof approach has been
  failing across ≥3 iters and the planner suspects a *structural*
  mismatch rather than a missing lemma. The progress-critic's
  STUCK / CHURNING verdicts with "approach-shape suspected" as a
  root cause are explicit signals.
- The strategy-critic suggested an alternative route but you don't
  have a concrete starting shape — ask me to find precedents for
  the alternative in any Mathlib area.
- A new infrastructure construction is hard and you want to see
  whether Mathlib has solved the SAME categorical / topological /
  algebraic shape in a different domain (presheaf on schemes vs
  presheaf on topological spaces; pushout in algebra vs in
  geometry; descent in algebraic geometry vs in functional analysis;
  fixed-point theorems across measure theory / order theory /
  topology; ...).

In cross-domain-inspiration mode, your directive replaces the
api-alignment fields with:

- `## Structural problem` — the categorical / algebraic / topological
  shape you're trying to solve, stated as abstractly as possible
  ("we need a sheaf condition for a locally trivializable functor of
  modules", "we need a descent statement for a stack property along
  a flat surjection", ...).
- `## Failed approaches` — one short bullet per attempt that didn't
  work, with the underlying obstacle.
- `## Search radius` — `wide` (any Mathlib domain) | `narrow` (same
  general area as the project, but a different sub-area).

My output in cross-domain-inspiration mode is a ranked list of
structural analogues — each with: the Mathlib citation, the
technique used there, and a concrete suggestion for how to port it
to the project's setting. The planner uses this as an exploration
list, not as a directive.

### Strict severity

When I find that Mathlib has a canonical idiom and the project chose
a parallel API anyway, I report this as critical, even if the
project's path works. "Works" is not the bar when the cost is API
fragmentation, bridge lemmas, and downstream code that can't consume
the unified Mathlib version.

I am NOT a sanity-check stamp. Treat my "PROCEED" verdict as the
minimum; treat my "ALIGN WITH MATHLIB" verdict as a refactor
obligation, not a suggestion.

I produce a persistent file under `analogies/<slug>.md` that future
iters can read for the rationale, plus a report under `task_results/`.

### Strict context discipline

Your directive must contain ONLY:

    iter's prover assignment (one block per route).

Your directive MUST NOT include:

  - Blueprint chapters (math correctness is the blueprint
    reviewer's territory).
  - Iter sidecars' full content (just the extracted signals named
    above).

If the directive includes content I am NOT supposed to see, ignore
it. My value depends on the narrow focus.

### Acting on my verdicts

Verdicts are per-route:

- **CONVERGING** — the route is closing. Proceed with the next
  prover round.
- **CHURNING** — each iter adds helpers but the residual hasn't
  shrunk. STOP assigning more helpers. My report names the
  corrective TYPE (blueprint expansion, Mathlib-idiom consult,
  structural refactor, route pivot); the planner picks the
  matching subagent from the catalog.
- **STUCK** — no sorry-elimination or structural advance in K
  iters. STOP this route; address the blocker or pivot.
- **UNCLEAR** — not enough signal yet (fresh route, 1-2 iters of
  data). Proceed but watch.

**CHURNING and STUCK are must-fix-this-iter.** The planner must
respond — either with the action I recommend, or with an explicit
rebuttal in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` naming why my read is wrong.
Silently assigning another helper round on a CHURNING route is the
failure pattern this subagent exists to prevent.

111	transcendental generator: `D (X) ≠ 0` in `Ω[Frac(k[X])⁄k]`. Verified route

118	private lemma _ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero {k : Type*} [Field k] :
119	    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
120	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) (FractionRing (Polynomial k)) Polynomial.X) ≠ 0 := by
121	  intro h
122	  have hmap : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
123	      (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
124	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (Polynomial k) Polynomial.X) = 0 := by
125	    rw [_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D]; exact h
126	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))
127	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
128	        (FractionRing (Polynomial k))) :=
129	    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map k (Polynomial k)
130	      (FractionRing (Polynomial k)) (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))
131	  rw [IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))] at hmap
132	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hmap
133	  rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hc
134	  have hc1 := congrArg (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv (R := k)) hc
135	  rw [map_smul, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv_D, Polynomial.derivative_X,
136	    map_zero, smul_eq_mul, mul_one] at hc1
137	  exact nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero c hc1

139	/-- (FT.3 closer) Over an algebraically closed base field `k`, an element of a
140	`k`-algebra field `K` that is algebraic over `k` lies in the image of
141	`algebraMap k K`. Verified route (analogist iter-154): `k⟮b⟯` is finite over `k`
142	(`IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional`), hence integral, so
143	`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` makes `algebraMap k k⟮b⟯`
144	surjective; pull the generator back. -/
145	private lemma _algebraic_mem_range {k K : Type*} [Field k] [Field K] [IsAlgClosed k]
146	    [Algebra k K] {b : K} (hb : IsAlgebraic k b) :
147	    b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range := by
148	  haveI : FiniteDimensional k k⟮b⟯ := IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional hb.isIntegral
149	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k k⟮b⟯ := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k k⟮b⟯
150	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
151	    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := k) (K := k⟮b⟯)).surjective
152	      (IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.gen k b)
153	  exact ⟨c, by
154	    have := congrArg (algebraMap k⟮b⟯ K) hc
155	    rwa [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.algebraMap_gen]
156	      at this⟩

158	/-- Algebra-level core (KDM): for `k` an algebraically closed field of
159	characteristic 0 (`[IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]`) and `B` a finite-type
160	`k`-algebra that is an integral domain (`[IsDomain B]`), if `b : B` satisfies
161	`D b = 0` in `Ω[B⁄k]` then `b ∈ range (algebraMap k B)`. This is the
162	chart-algebra (β-core ring-side) helper of the iter-144 piece (ii) pivot,
163	formalising the "constants = kernel of the universal derivation" content of
164	the Kähler derivation in characteristic 0.
165	
166	The JOINT `[IsAlgClosed k]` + `[IsDomain B]` hypotheses are essential — the
167	bare `B`-only form is FALSE (counterexamples: `B = k × k` killed by
168	`[IsDomain B]`; `B = ℚ(√2)/ℚ` killed by `[IsAlgClosed k]`).
169	
170	CLOSED iter-154 via the verified single-element / perfect-field /
171	Jacobi–Zariski `H1Cotangent` route (analogist `ftthree-kernel`,
172	`analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md`; blueprint `RigidityKbar.tex` § FT):
173	* (FT.1) push `D_B b = 0` to the fraction field `K = Frac B` via
174	  `KaehlerDifferential.map_D`; it suffices to put `algebraMap B K b` in
175	  `range (algebraMap k K)` and pull back along the injective `B ↪ K`;
176	* (FT.2) reduce to `IsAlgebraic k (algebraMap B K b)`: if it were
177	  transcendental, embed `F = Frac(k[X]) ↪ K` (`X ↦ bK`); char-0 ⟹
178	  `PerfectField F`, and `K` is `EssFiniteType` over `F`, so
179	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` gives `FormallySmooth F K`, hence
180	  `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent F K)`; the Jacobi–Zariski exact sequence
181	  (`Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange`) then makes `mapBaseChange k F K`
182	  injective, and `mapBaseChange (1 ⊗ D_F X_F) = D_K bK = 0` ⟹ (faithful
183	  flatness, `Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff`) `D_F X_F = 0`;
184	* (FT.3) the base case `_ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero` (`D_F X ≠ 0` via the localized
185	  Kähler module + `polynomialEquiv_D`) contradicts that, so `bK` is algebraic;
186	  `_algebraic_mem_range` (alg-closed `k`) then lands `bK ∈ range`.
187	
188	The standard-smooth / relative-dimension hypotheses (`n`,
189	`[IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]`) are NOT used by this route —
190	only `[IsDomain B]` + `[Algebra.FiniteType k B]` to build `K` and the
191	`EssFiniteType k K` chain — but they are retained on the signature (frozen,
192	harmless) for the downstream consumer.
193	
194	Reverse inclusion: `_hRev` records that `D ∘ algebraMap k B = 0` so the
195	forward direction stated here is the substantive content. Downstream
196	consumer: `GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` below. -/
197	theorem KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero
198	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]
199	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [IsDomain B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
200	    {n : ℕ} [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]
201	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
202	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range := by
203	  classical
204	  -- Reverse inclusion (recorded for downstream symmetry):
205	  -- `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` vanishes on the algebraMap image by
206	  -- `Derivation.map_algebraMap`.
207	  have _hRev : ∀ a : k, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B (algebraMap k B a) = 0 :=
208	    fun a => (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B).map_algebraMap a
209	  -- ===========================================================================
210	  -- iter-154 closure: the verified single-element / perfect-field /
211	  -- Jacobi–Zariski `H1Cotangent` route (analogist `ftthree-kernel`,
212	  -- `analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md`). The standard-smooth structure is
213	  -- NOT used by this argument (only `[IsDomain B]` + `[FiniteType k B]` to
214	  -- build `K = Frac B` and `EssFiniteType k K`); `n` and standard-smoothness
215	  -- remain harmless ambient hypotheses.
216	  -- ===========================================================================
217	  -- (FT.1) Push `D_B b = 0` forward to the fraction field `K = Frac B`.
218	  set K := FractionRing B with hK
219	  have hDbK : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k K (algebraMap B K b) = 0 := by
220	    rw [← _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D k k B K b, hDb, map_zero]
221	  -- It suffices to put `algebraMap B K b ∈ range(algebraMap k K)`; pull back
222	  -- along the injective `algebraMap B K` (domain ↪ fraction field).
223	  suffices hbK : algebraMap B K b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range by
224	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hbK
225	    refine ⟨c, ?_⟩
226	    apply IsFractionRing.injective B K
227	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k B K]
228	    exact hc
229	  -- Reduce to algebraicity of `bK := algebraMap B K b` over `k`, then close
230	  -- with `_algebraic_mem_range` (alg-closed `k`).
231	  apply _algebraic_mem_range
232	  -- (FT.2) Suppose, for contradiction, that `bK` is transcendental over `k`.
233	  by_contra hCon
234	  have hTr : Transcendental k (algebraMap B K b) := hCon
235	  have hInj : Function.Injective ⇑(Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)) :=
236	    transcendental_iff_injective.mp hTr
237	  -- Embed `F = Frac(k[X])` into `K` by `X ↦ bK` via `IsFractionRing.lift`.
238	  set F := FractionRing (Polynomial k) with hF
239	  have hg : Function.Injective ⇑((Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)).toRingHom) := hInj
240	  letI algFK : Algebra F K := (IsFractionRing.lift hg).toAlgebra
241	  have hψ : ∀ x : Polynomial k,
242	      algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F x) =
243	        (Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)).toRingHom x :=
244	    fun x => IsFractionRing.lift_algebraMap hg x
245	  -- Scalar tower `k → F → K`: `algebraMap F K ∘ algebraMap k F = algebraMap k K`.
246	  haveI : IsScalarTower k F K := by
247	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (fun x => ?_)
248	    rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k (Polynomial k) F x, hψ]
249	    change algebraMap k K x =
250	      (Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)) (algebraMap k (Polynomial k) x)
251	    rw [Polynomial.algebraMap_eq, Polynomial.aeval_C]
252	  -- `K` is essentially of finite type over `F` (via `EssFiniteType k K`).
253	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k K := Algebra.EssFiniteType.comp k B (FractionRing B)
254	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType F K := Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_comp k F K
255	  -- `F` is a perfect field (char 0) ⟹ `K/F` formally smooth ⟹ `H¹` vanishes.
256	  haveI : PerfectField F := PerfectField.ofCharZero
257	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth F K := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField
258	  -- (FT.2 core) `mapBaseChange k F K` is injective via the Jacobi–Zariski
259	  -- exact sequence + `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent F K)` from formal smoothness.
260	  have hMapInj : Function.Injective (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange k F K) := by
261	    rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
262	    intro x hx
263	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := (Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange k F K x).mp hx
264	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
265	  -- `algebraMap F K (image of X) = bK`.
266	  have hbKeq : algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) =
267	      algebraMap B K b := by
268	    rw [hψ]; exact Polynomial.aeval_X _
269	  -- `mapBaseChange (1 ⊗ D_F X_F) = D_K (algebraMap F K X_F) = D_K bK = 0`.
270	  have hident : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange k F K
271	      (1 ⊗ₜ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
272	        (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X)) =
273	      _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k K
274	        (algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X)) := by
275	    rw [_root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange_tmul, one_smul,
276	      _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D]
277	  have h1tmul : (1 ⊗ₜ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
278	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) : K ⊗[F] Ω[F⁄k]) = 0 := by
279	    apply hMapInj
280	    rw [map_zero, hident, hbKeq]
281	    exact hDbK
282	  -- Faithful flatness of the field extension `F → K` cancels the `1 ⊗ -`.
283	  have hDFX : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
284	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) = 0 :=
285	    (Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff F (Ω[F⁄k]) _).mp h1tmul
286	  -- (FT.3 base case) But `D_F X_F ≠ 0` — contradiction.
287	  exact _ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero hDFX

337	/-- Integrally-closed-constants helper: in a (smooth) proper geometrically
338	irreducible scheme `X` over a base field `k`, the global sections
339	`Γ(X, O_X)` are exhausted by the image of the structure-morphism's
340	action on the top open, i.e.\ the `appTop` ring hom of the structure
341	morphism `X ⟶ Spec k` is surjective. Equivalently, `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` as
342	`k`-algebras (modulo the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≃ k`).
343	
344	Blueprint: `chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
345	`\lem:constants_integral_over_base_field`. CLOSED iter-153 under the
346	iter-152 alg-closed pivot (`[IsAlgClosed k]`); the proof is three steps:
347	(1) `IsReduced X` + `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` (over the
348	    singleton-base `Spec k`) ⇒ `IrreducibleSpace X`
349	    (`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`) ⇒
350	    `IsIntegral X` (`isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`);
351	    `IsProper` ⇒ `UniversallyClosed` ⇒ `Γ(X, O_X)` is a field
352	    (`isField_of_universallyClosed`).
353	(2) `IsProper` (⇒ `UniversallyClosed` + `LocallyOfFiniteType`) ⇒ the
354	    structure-morphism `appTop` ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` is finite
355	    (`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`), hence `Γ(X, O_X)` is
356	    integral over `k`.
357	(3) `k` algebraically closed ⇒ an integral extension is trivial:
358	    `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives
359	    `algebraMap k Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective, i.e.
360	    `RingHom.range (appTop.hom) = ⊤`. (Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the
361	    former base-change-to-`\bar k` substep collapses entirely — no
362	    `ChartAlgebraS3` separability/inseparability factorisation is used.)
363	
364	The `[IsReduced X]` hypothesis is added explicitly because Mathlib
365	snapshot `b80f227` lacks the lemma `Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` over a field —
366	see the `Rigidity.lean` "Hypothesis history" block for the same
367	explicit-`IsReduced` discipline. -/
368	theorem constants_integral_over_base_field
369	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] {X : Scheme.{u}} [X.Over (Spec (.of k))]
370	    [IsProper (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
371	    [Smooth (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
372	    [IsReduced X]
373	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec (.of k))] :
374	    RingHom.range ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom) = ⊤ := by
375	  -- Iter-153 alg-closed closure. Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the proof
376	  -- collapses to three steps: (1) `X` integral ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` a field;
377	  -- (2) properness ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` finite (hence integral) over `k`;
378	  -- (3) `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives the
379	  -- structure ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective,
380	  -- which is the displayed `range = ⊤`.
381	  set f := X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) with hf
382	  -- (1) `X` is integral: smoothness gives `IsReduced X`, geometric
383	  -- irreducibility over the singleton base `Spec k` gives `IrreducibleSpace X`,
384	  -- and the two combine to `IsIntegral X`.
385	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X :=
386	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton f
387	  haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X
388	  -- (1, cont.) properness ⟹ universally closed ⟹ `Γ(X, ⊤)` is a field.
389	  letI : Field Γ(X, ⊤) := (isField_of_universallyClosed k f).toField
390	  -- (2) The composite `F : k → Γ(X, ⊤)` (structure map through the
391	  -- canonical `ΓSpecIso`) is finite, hence integral over `k`.
392	  let F := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ f.appTop
393	  have hFinF : F.hom.Finite := by
394	    apply RingHom.finite_respectsIso.2
395	      (e := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv)
396	    exact finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k f
397	  letI : Algebra k Γ(X, ⊤) := F.hom.toAlgebra
398	  haveI : Module.Finite k Γ(X, ⊤) := hFinF
399	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k Γ(X, ⊤) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k Γ(X, ⊤)
400	  -- (3) `k` algebraically closed ⟹ `algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤) = F.hom` is
401	  -- bijective, hence surjective.
402	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤)) :=
403	    IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral
404	  -- Convert the range goal to surjectivity of `f.appTop.hom`; since
405	  -- `F.hom = f.appTop.hom ∘ (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom` is surjective, so is
406	  -- `f.appTop.hom`.
407	  rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]
408	  have hFsurj : Function.Surjective ⇑F.hom := hbij.surjective
409	  have hcomp : F.hom = f.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom := rfl
410	  rw [hcomp, RingHom.coe_comp] at hFsurj
411	  exact hFsurj.of_comp

419	is separated over `Spec k`, `C.left` is reduced, and `C` is
420	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k`.

423	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` (in

1	# Analogy: kernel of the universal Kähler derivation = field of constants (FT.3)
2	
3	## Mode
4	cross-domain-inspiration
5	
6	## Slug
7	ftthree-kernel
8	
9	## Iteration
10	154
11	
12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	
14	For a separably-generated (here char-0) field extension `K / k`, the kernel of
15	the universal derivation `d : K → Ω_{K/k}` is the relative algebraic closure of
16	`k` in `K`. Concretely: `d x = 0 ⟹ x algebraic over k`. Combined with the
17	standing "`k` algebraically closed in `K`" (from `[IsAlgClosed k] + [IsDomain B]`,
18	`K = Frac B`), this gives `x ∈ k`. The content lives in the *transcendental*
19	layer (`Ω_{K/k} ≠ 0`), so unramified/`Ω=0` arguments do not reach it.
20	
21	The concrete Lean residual is the single `sorry` in
22	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean:427`
23	(`KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`), stated on the
24	standard-smooth domain `B` (not yet on `K`): `D_B b = 0 ⟹ b ∈ range(algebraMap k B)`.
25	
26	## Failed approaches (from directive)
27	- `Differential.ContainConstants` / `mem_range_of_deriv_eq_zero`: abstract single
28	  derivation `B → B`, only trivial `ContainConstants A A` instance ships.
29	- `FormallyUnramified.iff_isSeparable` + `Subsingleton Ω[L/K]`: only the algebraic
30	  (`EssFiniteType`, `Ω=0`) layer; misses the transcendental content.
31	- `Smooth.Field` (`FormallySmooth.of_algebraicIndependent_of_isSeparable`): gives
32	  formal smoothness but exposes no `Ω`-basis from a separating transcendence basis
33	  and no kernel description.
34	- `loogle "KaehlerDifferential.D _ _ ?x = 0"` → ∅: no shipped kernel-of-`D` lemma.
35	
36	## HEADLINE FINDING (overturns iter-153 "genuine Mathlib gap" verdict)
37	
38	FT.3 has **no single shipped kernel-of-`d` lemma**, but **every structural
39	ingredient to assemble it IS in Mathlib `b80f227`**, and a *cleaner route than
40	the directive's 4-step separating-transcendence-basis assembly* exists. I
41	verified the full chain by compilation via `lean_run_code` (each `example`
42	below type-checks against the project toolchain). FT.3 is **assemblable project
43	material (~100–150 LOC)**, NOT a Mathlib theory gap. The iter-153 bright-line
44	STOP should be lifted; re-dispatch a prover with the route below.
45	
46	The decisive move is to **stop trying to describe `ker d` directly** and instead
47	realize the missing *injectivity of the cotangent base-change* via the
48	**Jacobi–Zariski / `H1Cotangent` exact sequence** + **formal smoothness of a
49	perfect-field (char-0) extension**. This is the "one shelf over" analogue: the
50	cotangent-complex API, not the derivation-kernel API.
51	
52	## Analogues found
53	
54	Ranked by porting cost (lowest first).
55	
56	### Analogue: `Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange` + `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`
57	
58	- **Domain**: cotangent complex / deformation theory (Jacobi–Zariski), crossed
59	  with field-theoretic formal smoothness.
60	- **Same structural problem there**: for a tower `k → F → K`, the sequence
61	  `H¹(L_{K/F}) --δ--> K ⊗_F Ω_{F/k} --mapBaseChange--> Ω_{K/k}` is exact
62	  (`Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange`,
63	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.JacobiZariski`/`CotangentComplex`). When `K/F` is
64	  formally smooth, `H¹(L_{K/F})` is `Subsingleton`
65	  (`instSubsingletonH1CotangentOfFormallySmooth`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic`),
66	  so `mapBaseChange k F K` is **injective**. This is exactly the directive's
67	  desired LEFT-exactness of `Ω_{F/k} ⊗ K → Ω_{K/k}`.
68	- **Why it applies without a transcendence basis**: take `F = k(b)`. In char 0,
69	  `F` is a **perfect field** (`PerfectField.ofCharZero`, `Mathlib.FieldTheory.Perfect`),
70	  and `K` is essentially of finite type over `F`
71	  (`Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_comp` from `EssFiniteType k K`,
72	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.EssentialFiniteType`). Then
73	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Field`)
74	  gives `FormallySmooth F K` directly — **no separating transcendence basis, no
75	  "all partials vanish" combinatorics, no freeness-of-Ω-on-a-basis step.**
76	- **Technique**: `mapBaseChange k F K (1 ⊗ D_F x) = D_K (algebraMap F K x)` via
77	  `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange_tmul` + `map_D` (`one_smul`). So
78	  `D_K b = 0 ⟹ 1 ⊗ D_F b = 0 ⟹ D_F b = 0` by faithful flatness
79	  (`Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff`; the instance `FaithfullyFlat F K`
80	  for a field extension resolves by `inferInstance`). Contradiction with the base
81	  case below ⟹ `b` is algebraic over `k`.
82	- **Mapping to project**: in
83	  `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`, first push `D_B b = 0`
84	  to `D_K b = 0` (`K = FractionRing B`) via `map_D` (the project already has this
85	  as `_hFunct`). Then run the contradiction with `F` realized as
86	  `FractionRing (Polynomial k)` embedded into `K` by `b` (see base case).
87	- **Porting cost**: **low–medium**. All lemmas verified to compose; the work is
88	  scalar-tower/instance plumbing.
89	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
90	
91	### Analogue: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` + `polynomialEquiv_D` (transcendental base case)
92	
93	- **Domain**: localization / essentially-étale base change of Kähler differentials.
94	- **Same structural problem there**: a localization `S → T` is formally étale
95	  (`Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isLocalization`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Basic`),
96	  so `Ω_{T/k}` is the localized module of `Ω_{S/k}`
97	  (`KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Localization`).
98	- **Technique (FULLY verified, ~20 LOC)**: to show `D_{k(t)} t ≠ 0` for `t`
99	  transcendental, take `S = k[X]`, `T = FractionRing k[X]`. From `D_T(t)=0`,
100	  `map k k S T (D_S X) = 0`, so by `IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff` there is
101	  `c ∈ nonZeroDivisors` with `c • D_S X = 0`. Apply the `k[X]`-linear iso
102	  `KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv` (`polynomialEquiv_D : polynomialEquiv (D X) = derivative X = 1`):
103	  `c • 1 = 0` in `k[X]`, contradicting `nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero`. No
104	  `NoZeroSMulDivisors` lemma even needed.
105	- **Mapping to project**: this is the only genuinely-new helper. Stated as
106	  `D_{FractionRing (Polynomial k)} (algebraMap _ _ X) ≠ 0`. To use it inside the
107	  main proof, embed `F := FractionRing (Polynomial k) (≅ RatFunc k)` into `K` via
108	  `IsFractionRing.lift` of the injective `k[X] → K`, `X ↦ b` (injective because
109	  `b` transcendental). Avoids `IntermediateField.adjoin` entirely.
110	- **Porting cost**: **low** (base case verified end-to-end).
111	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
112	
113	### Analogue (discarded): separating-transcendence-basis freeness of `Ω`
114	
115	- Mathlib has `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.rank_kaehlerDifferential` and
116	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
117	  (`= n`), and `Algebra.trdeg`, but **no `IsTranscendenceBasis`-keyed
118	  freeness-of-`Ω` lemma** and **no general `rank Ω_{K/k} = trdeg`**. The
119	  directive's steps 1–2 (choose separating transcendence basis, `Ω` free on it)
120	  would have to be built. The `H1Cotangent` route makes this unnecessary —
121	  **discard**.
122	
123	## Top suggestion
124	
125	Build FT.3 by the **single-element / perfect-field route**, not the 4-step
126	transcendence-basis route. Concretely, in
127	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`, replace the `sorry` at L427 with:
128	
129	1. A new private helper `_ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero` (≈20 LOC, **verified**):
130	   `D_{FractionRing (Polynomial k)} (algebraMap _ _ X) ≠ 0`, via
131	   `isLocalizedModule_map` + `IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff` + `polynomialEquiv_D`.
132	2. The main reduction `D_B b = 0 ⟹ IsAlgebraic k b` (≈60–90 LOC): push to
133	   `K = FractionRing B`; `by_contra` ⟹ `Transcendental k b` ⟹ `k[X] → K` (`X↦b`)
134	   injective ⟹ `IsFractionRing.lift` gives a field embedding `F := RatFunc k → K`;
135	   register `Algebra F K` + `IsScalarTower k F K`; `FormallySmooth F K`
136	   (`of_perfectField`, using `PerfectField.ofCharZero` + `EssFiniteType.of_comp`);
137	   `mapBaseChange k F K` injective (via `exact_δ_mapBaseChange` + `Subsingleton H1`);
138	   `D_K b = mapBaseChange (1 ⊗ D_F X_F)` ⟹ `1 ⊗ D_F X_F = 0` ⟹ (`FaithfullyFlat F K`,
139	   `one_tmul_eq_zero_iff`) `D_F X_F = 0`, contradicting step 1.
140	3. Close with `b` algebraic ⟹ `b ∈ range(algebraMap k B)` (≈10 LOC, **verified**):
141	   `adjoin.finiteDimensional` + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`
142	   (the project already uses the latter in `constants_integral_over_base_field`).
143	
144	First file to touch: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` (the `_mvPoly_*`
145	free-case helpers become dead and may be removed — they targeted the abandoned
146	transcendence-basis/joint-pderiv route). The `[IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]
147	[IsDomain B] [FiniteType k B] [IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]`
